perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and not for any third party’s benefit.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party for any purpose they deem necessary, including third party’s benefit.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly stating that each party can use confidential information for 'any purpose they deem necessary, including third party’s benefit.' This directly contradicts the rest of the clause which explicitly states that each party needs to use the confidential information solely for the 'Authorized Purpose and not for any third party's benefit.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party for any purpose they deem necessary, including third party’s benefit. Each party agrees to limit disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to those employees and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions regarding the Authorized Purpose. Each party agrees that it will not disclose any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the express written consent of Owner. Neither party shall reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any of the Confidential Information of the other party or any of the prototypes, software or other tangible objects which embody the Confidential Information of the other party and which are provided to the party hereunder. Certain Confidential Information may be considered material non-public information under Regulation FD promulgated by the Securities and Exchange Commission. Recipient acknowledges that the United States securities laws prohibit it or any person who has received material nonpublic information about the Owner from purchasing or selling securities of the Owner or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance on such information, and agrees not to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information until such time as the information becomes disseminated to the public either through a press release issued by Owner or through a filing made by Owner pursuant to the Securities CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 1 of 3 Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; provided, however, that Recipient may make any disclosure or use of such information to which Owner gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that each party can use confidential information 'for any purpose they deem necessary, including third party’s benefit.' This directly and explicitly contradicts the 'Non-use and Non-disclosure' section, which states that each party agrees to limit disclosures and not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without express written consent. This conflict creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text permits actions that the contradicted text prohibits, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that the United States securities laws prohibit it or any person who has received material nonpublic information about the Owner from purchasing or selling securities of the Owner or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance on such information, and agrees not to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information until such time as the information becomes disseminated to the public either through a press release issued by Owner or through a filing made by Owner pursuant to the Securities CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 1 of 3 Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; provided, however, that Recipient may make any disclosure or use of such information to which Owner gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the United States securities laws prohibit it or any person who has received material nonpublic information about the Owner from purchasing or selling securities of the Owner or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance on such information, and agrees to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information until such time as the information becomes disseminated to the public either through a press release issued by Owner or through a filing made by Owner pursuant to the Securities CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 1 of 3 Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; provided, however, that Recipient may make any disclosure or use of such information to which Owner gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the section 4 creates an in-text contradiction as it stipulates that the recipient 'agrees to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information', even though the previous clause explicitly states the recipient 'agrees not to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and not for any third party’s benefit. Each party agrees to limit disclosures of Confidential Information of the other party to those employees and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions regarding the Authorized Purpose. Each party agrees that it will not disclose any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the express written consent of Owner. Neither party shall reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any of the Confidential Information of the other party or any of the prototypes, software or other tangible objects which embody the Confidential Information of the other party and which are provided to the party hereunder. Certain Confidential Information may be considered material non-public information under Regulation FD promulgated by the Securities and Exchange Commission. Recipient acknowledges that the United States securities laws prohibit it or any person who has received material nonpublic information about the Owner from purchasing or selling securities of the Owner or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance on such information, and agrees to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information until such time as the information becomes disseminated to the public either through a press release issued by Owner or through a filing made by Owner pursuant to the Securities CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 1 of 3 Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; provided, however, that Recipient may make any disclosure or use of such information to which Owner gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text stated the recipient 'agrees not to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information', while the changed text states the recipient 'agrees to purchase, sell or otherwise engage in transactions in Owner’s stock based on such material nonpublic information'. This direct conflict creates a strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as a clause is placed with conflicting implications within the same section. As a result, the contradicted text is the same as the Changed text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires five (5) years from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”).
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires one (1) year from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”).
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the 'Confidentiality Term' from five years to one year. This contradicts the later part of Section 10 which states, 'each party’s obligation to keep the Confidential Information of the other party confidential for the Confidentiality Term shall apply'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding how long the confidentiality term lasts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires one (1) year from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”). Either party may terminate this Agreement earlier by giving thirty (30) days prior written notice of termination to the other party. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the obligations of each party shall survive with respect to Confidential Information of the other party disclosed hereunder until such time as the respective Confidential Information becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of Recipient or until the end of the Confidentiality Term, whichever occurs sooner. For the avoidance of doubt, upon termination of this Agreement, each party’s obligation to keep the Confidential Information of the other party confidential for the Confidentiality Term shall apply even in the event where one party is acquired or merged by or into a third party, and such third party shall have the right to enforce this obligation as a third party beneficiary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Confidentiality Term' from five years to one year. The contradicted text references this 'Confidentiality Term'. Since the 'Confidentiality Term' has been modified from five years to one, this contradicts the contradicted text regarding how long the confidentiality term lasts, creating an in-text contradiction within the same document.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement earlier by giving thirty (30) days prior written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement earlier by giving ninety (90) days prior written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The modification extends the notice period for termination from 30 to 90 days. This directly contradicts Section 12, which requires notices to be properly given if delivered by specific methods 'to the recipient at the address identified in its signature block to this Agreement'. Requiring 90 days of written notice directly affects the period to make those written exchanges. This adds uncertainty to which time period, 30 or 90 days, would apply and creates a conflict with the logistics for sending the notice in section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice. Any notice or other communication under this Agreement given by either party to the other party shall be deemed to be properly given if given in writing and delivered (i) by nationally recognized private courier (e.g., Federal Express), (ii) facsimile directed at the signatory of the other party (at the number below), or (iii) by mail (return receipt requested), properly addressed and stamped with the required postage, to the recipient at the address identified in its signature block to this Agreement. Either party may from time to time change its fax number or address by giving the other party notice of the change in accordance with this Section. CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 2 of 3
perturbation.justification: The change from 'thirty (30) days' to 'ninety (90) days' in the termination notice period directly contradicts the contradicted text. Section 12, defining how notice is given, implicitly relies on a reasonable timeframe for delivery and receipt, which the original 30-day period fulfills. Extending the notice period to 90 days directly affects the logistics for sending the notice in section 12 and adds uncertainty to which time period would apply, creating a conflict with the logistics for sending the notice in section 12, adhering to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision.
perturbation.changed_text: Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction within Section 13. By stating that 'Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver thereof' it creates direct in-text contradiction with the original sentence 'Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision'. This creates confusion on whether or not waiving the enforcement of the agreement is allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. This document contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and neither party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other party except as set forth herein. Each party represents, warrants and covenants that it has the full right and authority to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations hereunder, that all required corporate approvals and authorizations have been obtained, and that, upon signature by its authorized representative listed below, this Agreement shall have been duly executed and be legally binding upon the respective party in all respects. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Washington, without reference to conflict of laws principles. Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver thereof. This Agreement may not be amended, nor any obligation waived, except by a writing signed by both parties hereto. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement through their duly authorized representatives as of the Effective Date. CARDIAC SCIENCE CORPORATION Signature: /s/ Mark Daniel Name: Mark Daniel Title: Controller Fax Number: 425-402-2012 Date: 7/27/10 For: Signature: Name: Title: Fax Number: E-mail Address: Date: CSI/Opto Circuits /s/ Joseph LaPorta Joseph LaPorta COO 262-798-5237 joseph.laporta@csiusa.com 6/25/10 CONFIDENTIAL NDA-CSC Standard Page 3 of 3
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the contradicted text. The original text states that failure to enforce a provision does NOT constitute a waiver, while the changed text says it DOES constitute a waiver. Because the contradicted text is the same section as where the change was made, this is a direct in-text contradiction, creating confusion and uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement.
